title: raoulia subsericea
intro: Raoulia subsericea, also known as the Bronze Fern or Bronze Fleeceflower, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Raoulia subsericea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal purposes, and is also used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
